title: Installation
description: CReact runs on Node.js and uses JSX with TypeScript. The fastest way to begin is the create command; the rest of this page sets a project up by hand.
quickstart_intro: Scaffold a new project already wired for CReact. It prompts for a memory backend — a file ledger, SQLite, or in-memory — and sets up TypeScript JSX, a dev script, and a starter app:
quickstart_next_intro: Then install dependencies and start the app in watch mode:
code_quickstart_next:
cd my-app
npm install
npm run dev
quickstart_outro: The starter is a durable counter that restarts on file changes. To wire a project up by hand instead, follow the rest of this page.
create_project_intro: Create a directory and initialize it:
code_init_project:
mkdir my-app && cd my-app
npm init -y
install_typescript_intro: Install TypeScript as a dev dependency:
configure_typescript_intro: Create a <0>tsconfig.json</0> with JSX configured for CReact:
tip_jsx_import_source: <0>jsxImportSource</0> tells TypeScript to use CReact's JSX runtime instead of React's, enabling <1><Component /></1> syntax.
add_scripts_intro: Update your <0>package.json</0> with a module type and run scripts:
create_entry_intro: Create <0>index.tsx</0>. It exports a default async function that calls <1>render()</1> with a root component, a Memory implementation, and a stack name. This is the real entry point of the durable-counter example app — FileMemory is a small JSON file ledger covered in <2>State and Memory</2>:
run_outro: Your CReact app is now running. The <0>--watch</0> flag restarts on file changes.
code_project_structure:
my-app/
├── index.tsx           # Entry point, exports default async function
├── src/
│   └── app.tsx         # Root component
├── .state/             # Persisted state ledger, auto-generated
├── package.json
└── tsconfig.json
project_files_outro: The entry point <0>index.tsx</0> exports a default async function that the <1>creact</1> CLI calls. This function calls <2>render()</2> with your root component, a <3>Memory</3> implementation, and a stack name.
